g.data-narration: Welcome to Emerging Cloud Software Environments - the next generation platforms revolutionizing application development.
Cloud computing has evolved from basic IaaS to advanced platforms. Emerging environments abstract infrastructure for pure focus on application logic.
g.text: Cloud computing evolved from IaaS to next-gen platforms enabling developers to
build, deploy, and scale applications efficiently. These environments abstract
infrastructure management, letting developers focus on application logic only.

g.data-narration: These environments are essential for agility, scalability, and cost efficiency. They enable faster development and improved application performance.
Serverless computing (FaaS) lets developers write functions executed in response to events without managing any servers.
Serverless has four key features: event-driven execution, automatic scaling, pay-per-use billing, and stateless function design.
Container orchestration platforms manage containerized applications at scale. Kubernetes leads adoption as the open-source standard.
Major cloud providers offer managed Kubernetes: AWS has ECS/EKS, Microsoft offers AKS, and Google provides GKE.
Edge computing brings computation closer to data sources, reducing latency and bandwidth. AWS Greengrass enables local Lambda execution.
g.text: Reduces latency and bandwidth | Example: AWS Greengrass

g.data-narration: Cloud providers offer specialized ML platforms like AWS SageMaker and Azure ML for streamlined model training and deployment workflows.
AWS leads in serverless (Lambda), Azure excels in enterprise (AKS), and GCP dominates in data/ML (Vertex AI). Each has unique strengths.
Modern programming models include event-driven architecture, container-native development with Docker, and Infrastructure-as-Code using Terraform.
Security in cloud follows a shared model: providers handle physical and infrastructure security while customers manage application and data protection.
g.text: Application security | Data protection | Access management

g.data-narration: Follow security best practices: principle of least privilege, secrets management, network segmentation, continuous monitoring, and regular audits.

Key takeaways: Serverless abstracts server management with event-driven auto-scaling, Kubernetes leads container orchestration, edge computing reduces latency, and security follows shared responsibility.
For exams: Understand serverless vs containers, memorize provider services, read code snippets, and focus on the shared responsibility model.
g.text: • Memorize key services from AWS, Azure, GCP
